news.title: World Cup 2026: Spain and Argentina meet in a final like no other with Donald Trump involved, winners' rings and a first-ever half-time show
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup final will be like no other. Spain and Argentina face off in New York/New Jersey in what should be a brilliant football contest - but surrounded by pomp, fanfare and controversy not seen in the previous 22 editions of this showpiece match… How long is it going to go on for? It's quite possible it will be the longest World Cup final ever. But even if extra-time and penalties aren't required, the match is going to run way beyond two hours.

news.title: Gianni Infantino unlikely to face IOC sanctions over Balogun red card scandal
news.lede: Donald Trump spoke to Fifa president before ban lifted. Human rights body FairSquare sent complaint to IOC. Gianni Infantino is poised to escape without sanction after complaints he breached rules on political neutrality in his dealings with Donald Trump over the Folarin Balogun affair. The International Olympic Committee this week received a formal complaint from the human rights organisation FairSquare regarding Infantino’s conduct after Trump revealed he had called the Fifa president asking him to review Balogun’s ban from the USA’s last-16 tie against Belgium.

news.title: Spain forced to cancel final training session before World Cup final
news.lede: The Spanish national team were on Saturday forced to cancel their planned outdoor training session. This came owing to adverse weather conditions. Luis de la Fuente’s troops are of course currently in the midst of final preparations for the grandest fixture in all of international football. After battling their way beyond European rivals France this week, La Roja have set up a World Cup final showdown with reigning champions Argentina.

news.title: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game
news.lede: Forty-eight teams, 104 matches - and you can predict them all across the BBC Sport website and app. A new predictor game is being introduced to cover this year's World Cup, allowing users to pick what they think will be the result from every match. The new game also features streaks, so make sure to check in each day and make your predictions. Players will also have the chance to enter a prize draw to win an official World Cup football signed by members of the BBC World Cup team.

news.title: Where is the next World Cup 2030? Which countries are hosting?
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. The 2030 World Cup will be unlike any previous edition of football's biggest tournament. For the first time in the competition's history, matches will be staged across three continents, with six countries sharing hosting duties to celebrate the centenary of the World Cup.

news.title: Latin America unites to cheer against Argentina in FIFA World Cup final
news.lede: Fans from Mexico to Brazil unite to cheer Yamal's Spain against Argentina and Messi, whom they call 'FIFA’s golden boy'.

news.title: Rodeo delays, refrigerated turf and lots of lorries: Why preparing a World Cup pitch is so tough
news.lede: Nottingham Forest's pitch performance manager travelled to Texas to oversee the laying of the 16th pitch at the summer's World Cup finals.
